title: Houses, land, assets allowed to be mine
subtitle: A present-tense affirmation for claiming the right to own significant physical wealth.
excerpt: A short affirmation practice from Day 25 of the MONEY program. Use to state the ownership claim directly, without the qualifications your mind likes to attach.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 25 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when property or physical assets feel like they are for other people — people with more security, more knowledge, or a different background.

content.text: An affirmation from Day 25 of the MONEY program. The list format (houses, land, assets) is deliberate. Saying each category separately gives the mind less room to agree with one and quietly veto the others.
Houses are allowed to be mine. Land is allowed to be mine. Assets are allowed to be mine.
Say it out loud three times, hand on heart. Pause after each line. If one of the three produces resistance, that is the one to sit with longest.
Adapted from Day 25 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
